revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a pixel-art game-development terrain-tile asset sprite sheet.

Hard canvas contract: source artwork is exactly 64x64 pixels, arranged as a strict 4x4 grid of 16 tiles. Each tile is exactly 16x16 pixels. Render as crisp low-resolution 16-bit SNES-style pixel art with no antialiasing blur, no soft painterly edges, no vector smoothness, and no visible grid lines, borders, tile IDs, labels, watermark, UI, or text. Every frame must stay fully inside its own 16x16 cell, same scale and perspective, filled edge to edge, fully opaque. Use solid #FF00FF magenta only for any truly empty background space, but the tile cells should be opaque terrain art with no alpha holes.

Subject: top-down-front mossy dry-stone retaining wall tileset made of tightly packed rounded river boulders, Japanese nozura-zumi / tamaishi style. Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, cheerful warm sunlight from top-left / slightly overhead. Each stone must be a smooth rounded ovoid cobble with strong 3D volume: bright sunlit highlight on the upper curve, midtone warm grey and grey-brown body with per-stone color variation, dark deep crevice shadows below and between stones. The wall must read as bumpy rounded rock, not flat. Add soft green moss and tiny weeds tucked only in crevices, cohesive palette, one-pixel dark outline where useful.

Row layout, left to right variants within each row, seamlessly tileable horizontally:
Row 1: COPING CAP. Light dressed flat capstones forming the plateau lip, lush deep-green grass along the very top edge. Include straight center cap, slightly chipped cap, grass-overlap cap, and broken/irregular cap variant. Capstones are flatter than wall stones but still pixel-shaded.
Row 2: UPPER WALL. Rounded boulders, lighter and sunlit. Include center, edge-breakup, corner/inner-corner suggestion, and chipped shoreline-like irregular variant. Avoid repeated square stamps.
Row 3: LOWER WALL. Rounded boulders darker, deeper crevice shadows, more moss. Include center, edge, corner/inner-corner, and broken wall texture variant.
Row 4: BASE. Boulders meeting grass at the foot with small rubble, soil and weeds. Include base center, base edge, base corner/grass overlap, and rubble-heavy decoration variant.

Tilemap quality: no checkerboard read in final view, no repeated identical square stamps, vary stone sizes, highlights, cracks, moss, weeds, edge breakup, and lighting while keeping the set cohesive and horizontally seamless. Think broad world-map terrain readability rather than close-up village props.

Strict exclusions: no characters, no animals, no birds, no companion creatures, no posts, no lanterns, no objects, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no logos, no letter emblems, no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna likenesses.
